key_insight: An agentic multi-LLM system can synthesize EHR evidence into multidimensional disease severity phenotypes that align with established T2D severity measures and clinical outcomes.
clinical_relevance: This suggests LLM-based severity scoring could help stratify patients beyond rule-based indices, potentially supporting research and risk enrichment workflows in chronic disease.
method: Two-phase agentic LLM framework (MOSAIC) built for severity phenotyping
Evaluated in type 2 diabetes on SyntheticMass EHR data using open-weight and closed-weight pipelines
Compared against algorithmic ground truths and outcomes including mortality and complications
evidence_signal: On SyntheticMass, open-weight MOSAIC closely matched the proprietary pipeline (weighted kappa 0.773) and showed moderate-to-fair agreement with established comparators while separating mortality and complication risk strata.
watch_item: Performance was demonstrated in synthetic T2D data, so real-world validation, generalizability, and safe deployment controls are still needed.
paper_title: Multi-Large Language Model Orchestrated Severity Assessment of Clinical Records (MOSAIC)
